title: Deduplicate clause-scan logic in lib.rs
description: validate_stmt (lib.rs:279-341) and collect_regex_patterns (lib.rs:385-425) are near-identical imperative while-loops: both scan a flat form slice, track `clause_start`/`i` indexes, set boolean flags on Tag forms, and then dispatch on Block boundaries. The only difference is the payload (validate vs. cache). This is duplicated state-machine logic in ~80 lines of near-identical code. Suggested rewrite: extract a helper `iter_clauses(stmt) -> impl Iterator<Item = &[Form]>` that yields each clause slice (the span between consecutive Block forms). Both functions can then iterate over clauses and dispatch their own payload without reimplementing the scan. Alternatively, a single `scan_clauses(stmt, has_regex, has_matches, on_clause: FnMut(&[Form], PatternKind))` abstraction could unify both callers.
